reasoning:
The BTFP expired on March 11, 2024, and its 1-year loans matured fully by March 11, 2025. This explains the massive drop in the WLCFLL series over early 2025, landing at 4,298M on March 12, 2025. With BTFP completely off the books, the remaining series comprises mostly Primary Credit (which is typically highly volatile but low), slowing decaying PPPLF, and Seasonal Credit. 
1) In the short term, any remaining bridge loans in Primary Credit taken out to cover BTFP repayment will swiftly be repaid, exerting continued downward pressure. Thus, Mar 23 and Apr 15 dates are very likely to see a decrease.
2) In the medium term (June/Sept), Seasonal Credit touches its peak, which could contribute a small positive bump (~$100-200M). However, the ongoing amortization of PPPLF and stabilizing Primary Credit should suppress this, though the probability approaches 0.5 out of uncertainty.
3) In the long term (March 2026), base runoff could be countered by an increasing reliance on the discount window if Quantitative Tightening causes reserve scarcity, increasing the chance of crossing the 4,298 baseline and pushing the probability toward 0.5.
